one_line: Last Mughal emperor of Delhi and poetic symbol of the 1857 uprising.

appearance.age_look: Looks around 75–80, thin and slightly stooped, with a frail but dignified build.
appearance.face: Long, narrow face with high cheekbones, deep-set almond eyes, prominent hooked nose, hollow cheeks, light brown complexion weathered with fine wrinkles, long white mustache and trimmed white beard; default expression is calm, introspective melancholy with a quiet, steady gaze.
appearance.hair: Mostly hidden under his turban, showing only a thin fringe of grey-white hair at the temples; wears a tall, cylindrical Mughal-style turban in deep crimson silk, wrapped neatly with a narrow gold edging and a single central emerald-green turban jewel at the front.
appearance.costume: Full-length Mughal court jama in rich deep green silk with subtle self-woven floral motifs, overlapping right-to-left and tied at the side with a narrow crimson sash; the jama has long, slightly flared sleeves and a knee-down skirt that opens slightly at the front, revealing off-white cotton churidar trousers tightly gathered at the ankles; over his shoulders he drapes a light cream pashmina shawl with a fine red and gold border, usually hanging more over his left arm; on his feet are soft brown leather mojari shoes with slightly upturned toes and simple stitching; around his waist over the sash, a modest dark-red leather belt with a small brass buckle hints at his diminished but lingering imperial status.
appearance.signature: A slender, slightly curved Indo-Persian shamshir with a plain steel blade and a simple dark-brown horn hilt, sheathed in an unornamented dark leather scabbard worn at his left side, which he rests his right hand on when standing.

ref_desc: Elderly, thin Bahadur Shah Zafar with a long wrinkled face, white mustache and beard, wearing a deep green silk Mughal jama, cream churidar and shawl, brown mojari shoes, a tall crimson turban with a green jewel, and a plain leather-sheathed shamshir at his left side.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Bahadur Shah Zafar as an elderly, thin Mughal emperor: long, narrow wrinkled face, high cheekbones, deep-set serious eyes, prominent nose, light brown weathered complexion, long white mustache and neat white beard, calm melancholic expression. His hair is mostly hidden, with only a grey-white fringe at the temples, under a tall cylindrical crimson silk turban with neat wraps, a narrow gold edge, and one central emerald-green jewel. He wears a rich deep green silk Mughal jama with subtle woven floral patterns, overlapping right to left, tied with a narrow crimson sash, the long sleeves slightly flared and the skirt falling below the knee, opening a bit to show off-white cotton churidar tightly gathered at the ankles. A light cream pashmina shawl with a fine red and gold border is draped over his shoulders, hanging more over his left arm. On his feet are soft brown leather mojari shoes with slightly upturned toes and simple stitching. Around his waist over the sash sits a simple dark-red leather belt with a small brass buckle. At his left side is his signature weapon: a slender, slightly curved shamshir with a plain steel blade and dark-brown horn hilt, in an unadorned dark leather scabbard that his right hand often rests upon. Emphasize consistent colors—deep green, crimson red, cream, gold, and brown—and show him from multiple angles to clearly display face, hair, turban, full costume, and shamshir.
